title: lactuca serriola f. integrifolia
intro: Lactuca serriola f. integrifolia, also known as wild lettuce or prickly lettuce, is a subspecies of the Lactuca serriola plant, which is a type of wild lettuce that belongs to the dandelion family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and sedative effects, which can help to alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation. The health benefits of Lactuca serriola f. integrifolia include its ability to act as a natural pain reliever and its potential to help reduce insomnia and promote a restful night's sleep. The herbal preparations of this plant include dried leaves, which can be made into teas or infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal extracts.
